And so, when I show them the workflow that, I believe Daniel built, which was taking session data from Bolt and translating that session data into more structured data and descriptions of the session written in English, and then analyzing that, his mind was completely blown.
And, like, it was clear he wanted it tomorrow, right?
And so, essentially, what the workflow does is, it looks at a bunch of, like, click data and session data.
So, Bolt gave us a dump of every session that went through and sign up, did some stuff, got stuck, went away and came back days later, did more stuff, and then paid, right?

So, we took that dump of cohort and went through this workflow that not only categorized that, but essentially described this user did this, this, and this, and then did this, and this is what happened, right?
And then that information enabled us to then cluster.

And come up with, like, key insights for Bolt, on which the insight was mostly a lot of the users that are getting success and monetizing are essentially developers that work for agencies that go in and try to build a website that gets stuff, come back and build a fully functioning website for clients, and then they pay, right?

And so, but you can imagine if you have millions and millions of sessions, like, making sense of those sessions, and finding kind of the needle, the haystack, or kind of, like, describing what those sessions are, is extremely valuable.

So that's just, like, one use case, and it's just a great example of how, like, these workflows, they seem like they're doing one very narrow thing, and then all of a sudden, like, that same thing can be applied for any website on the planet, you know?
